"""Run a single query and return whether the skill was triggered.

    Creates a command file in .claude/commands/ so it appears in Claude's
    available_skills list, then runs `claude -p` with the raw query.
    Uses --include-partial-messages to detect triggering early from
    stream events (content_block_start) rather than waiting for the
    full assistant message, which only arrives after tool execution.
    """
applied.new_excerpt:
"""Run a single query and return whether the skill was triggered.

    Invokes `hermes chat -q <query> --output-format json` and then exports
    the resulting session via `hermes sessions export --session-id <sid>`.
    Trigger detection scans the ShareGPT-flavored JSONL for assistant turns
    containing a `Skill` tool_use block whose `arguments.skill` matches the
    candidate skill name (matching the cleaned `skill_name-eval-<id>` form).
    """

In Claude.ai, the core workflow is the same (draft → test → review → improve → repeat), but because Claude.ai doesn't have subagents, some mechanics change. Here's what to adapt:

**Running test cases**: ...
**Reviewing results**: ...

## Cowork-Specific Instructions

If you're in Cowork, the main things to know are:

- You have subagents, so the main workflow (spawn test cases in parallel, run baselines, grade, etc.) all works. (However, if you run into severe problems with timeouts, it's OK to run the test prompts in series rather than parallel.)
- ...
- **Updating an existing skill**: The user might be asking you to update an existing skill, not create a new one. Follow the update guidance in the claude.ai section above.

applied.old_excerpt:
# Grader Agent

Evaluate expectations against an execution transcript and outputs.

## Role
applied.new_excerpt:
---
name: grader
description: Evaluate expectations against an execution transcript and outputs, then critique the evals themselves for discriminating power.
goal: Grade every expectation against transcript + outputs and surface eval-quality feedback where assertions are weak or missing.
toolsets:
  - file_read
  - file_write
role: leaf
---

## delegate_task invocation

```
delegate_task(
    goal="Grade all expectations for the run and flag weak assertions",
    context="transcript_path, outputs_dir, expectations list",
    toolsets=["file_read", "file_write"],
    role="leaf",
    max_iterations=5,
)
```

# Grader Agent

Evaluate expectations against an execution transcript and outputs.

## Role

applied.old_excerpt:
# Post-hoc Analyzer Agent

Analyze blind comparison results to understand WHY the winner won and generate improvement suggestions.

## Role
applied.new_excerpt:
---
name: analyzer
description: Analyze blind comparison results to extract WHY the winner won and produce prioritized improvement suggestions for the loser.
goal: Produce a structured analysis report comparing the winning and losing skill executions, with actionable improvement suggestions.
toolsets:
  - file_read
  - file_write
role: leaf
---

## delegate_task invocation

```
delegate_task(
    goal="Unblind the comparison and explain why the winner won, with prioritized improvements for the loser",
    context="winner, winner_skill_path, winner_transcript_path, loser_skill_path, loser_transcript_path, comparison_result_path, output_path",
    toolsets=["file_read", "file_write"],
    role="leaf",
    max_iterations=5,
)
```

# Post-hoc Analyzer Agent

Analyze blind comparison results to understand WHY the winner won and generate improvement suggestions.

## Role

applied.old_excerpt:
# Blind Comparator Agent

Compare two outputs WITHOUT knowing which skill produced them.

## Role
applied.new_excerpt:
---
name: comparator
description: Blindly compare two outputs (labeled A and B) and pick a winner based on rubric scores and optional expectation pass rates.
goal: Judge which of two outputs better accomplishes the eval task without knowing which skill produced each.
toolsets:
  - file_read
  - file_write
role: leaf
---

## delegate_task invocation

```
delegate_task(
    goal="Compare outputs A and B blindly and pick a winner based on output quality",
    context="output_a_path, output_b_path, eval_prompt, expectations (optional)",
    toolsets=["file_read", "file_write"],
    role="leaf",
    max_iterations=5,
)
```

# Blind Comparator Agent

Compare two outputs WITHOUT knowing which skill produced them.

## Role

applied.new_excerpt: NEW FILE: hermes_subprocess_env(extra_vars_to_strip: Iterable[str] | None = None) -> dict[str, str] — preserves all env vars by default; does NOT strip CLAUDECODE (Hermes does not auto-strip it because it is an Anthropic-specific signal); HERMES_SESSION_ID is read-only so never override; for nested Hermes use profile mechanism (hermes -p <name>) or override HERMES_HOME.

"configuration": "with_skill",

... "without_skill": { ... },

... `with_skill` / `without_skill`: Each contains `pass_rate`, `time_seconds`, `tokens` objects with `mean` and `stddev` fields
